column4: Title
column5: Bug Description
column9: AI Solution

column4: JWT Login Blocked - 503 Service Unavailable Despite Valid Token
column5: JWT is denying login and returning a 503 error even though authentication is successful and the user is active.
column9: Check if the backend server is running and reachable. Verify ALLOWED_HOSTS in Django settings.

column4: JWT Token Expiry Not Handled - Silent Logout on Frontend
column5: JWT access token expires but the frontend does not catch the 401 response or trigger a refresh.
column9: Add an Axios interceptor to catch 401 responses and automatically call the refresh token endpoint.

column4: JWT Refresh Token Reuse After Invalidation
column5: Refresh token continues to generate new access tokens even after the user has logged out.
column9: Enable token blacklisting in simplejwt. Call BlacklistView on logout. Set ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS=True.

column4: JWT Secret Key Mismatch Between Services
column5: Backend microservices using different SECRET_KEY values causing token signature verification to fail.
column9: Use a shared SECRET_KEY stored in environment variables and load consistently across all services.

column4: JWT Token Not Attached to Request Headers
column5: Frontend not correctly attaching the Bearer token in the Authorization header for protected API calls.
column9: In Axios set default headers or use an interceptor to attach the token before every request.

column4: JWT Algorithm Set to None - Security Vulnerability
column5: JWT accepting tokens signed with none algorithm due to missing algorithm validation in decode configuration.
column9: Explicitly set ALGORITHM=HS256 in simplejwt settings. Never allow none algorithm.

column4: JWT Payload Contains Sensitive User Data
column5: User email and password hash embedded directly in JWT payload without encryption.
column9: Only store non-sensitive identifiers in JWT payload. Never include passwords or emails.

column4: JWT Token Issued in Future - Clock Skew Issue
column5: Server and client clocks out of sync causing iat timestamp to appear in the future.
column9: Sync server time using NTP. Set LEEWAY in simplejwt settings to tolerate minor clock differences.

column4: JWT Blacklist Not Enforced on Logout
column5: After logout the invalidated JWT is still accepted because there is no token blacklist implemented.
column9: Add token_blacklist to INSTALLED_APPS. Use simplejwt BlacklistView for logout endpoint.

column4: JWT Role Claim Not Verified - Unauthorized Access to Admin Routes
column5: Role claim inside JWT payload not validated on protected admin endpoints.
column9: Create a custom permission class that reads the role claim. Apply custom HasRole permission on admin endpoints.

column4: JWT Login Blocked by IsAuthenticated on Login Endpoint
column5: The login endpoint has permission_classes=[IsAuthenticated] causing unauthenticated users to get 401.
column9: Change the login view to use permission_classes=[AllowAny].

column4: Global IsAuthenticated Setting Blocking Public Login Route
column5: DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES set to IsAuthenticated globally protecting the login endpoint.
column9: On every public endpoint explicitly override with permission_classes=[AllowAny] at the view level.

column4: JWT Token Not Generated Because Permission Check Fails Before Authentication
column5: Server runs permission check before processing login credentials returning 403.
column9: Set permission_classes=[AllowAny] on the login view. Also set authentication_classes=[].

column4: Login Returns 403 Instead of 401 Due to Wrong Permission Class
column5: Login view uses IsAdminUser instead of AllowAny returning 403 to all regular users.
column9: Replace IsAdminUser with AllowAny on the login endpoint.

column4: Inactive User Account Returns 200 But JWT Token Is Invalid
column5: User with is_active=False passes AllowAny check and receives a token that is rejected on subsequent requests.
column9: Explicitly check user.is_active before issuing a token. Return 403 with clear message.

column4: JWT Login Endpoint Accidentally Wrapped with IsAuthenticated Decorator
column5: @permission_classes([IsAuthenticated]) decorator applied on login view overriding AllowAny.
column9: Replace the decorator with @permission_classes([AllowAny]) on the login view function.

column4: AllowAny Set on Login View But JWT Authentication Class Still Rejects Request
column5: AllowAny is set but DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES includes JWTAuthentication globally.
column9: On the login view also set authentication_classes=[] to bypass JWT authentication entirely.

column4: JWT Login Fails in Production Due to Missing AllowAny in Production Settings
column5: Production settings override DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES without login endpoint override.
column9: Always explicitly set permission_classes=[AllowAny] on the view itself not relying on settings defaults.

column4: JWT Login Fails After Middleware Enforces Authentication Before View Is Reached
column5: Custom authentication middleware checks JWT on every request. Login endpoint never receives request.
column9: Update middleware to whitelist public paths before applying JWT validation.

column4: Superuser Can Login But Normal Users Get 401 Due to Mixed Permission Classes
column5: Login view has permission_classes=[IsAdminUser|IsAuthenticated] blocking non-admin users.
column9: Replace mixed permission class with permission_classes=[AllowAny] on the login endpoint.

column4: Expired JWT Token Not Properly Handled
column5: The application fails to handle expired JWT tokens gracefully.
column9: Catch token expiration exceptions and return HTTP 401.

column4: Missing JWT Authentication Class
column5: JWT authentication is not configured in the application settings.
column9: Add the appropriate JWT authentication class.

column4: Invalid JWT Signature Verification
column5: JWT signatures are not properly verified before granting access.
column9: Validate token signatures using the correct secret and algorithm.

column4: Refresh Token Rotation Disabled
column5: Refresh tokens remain valid after reuse.
column9: Enable refresh token rotation and blacklisting.

column4: JWT Secret Key Hardcoded in Source Code
column5: Sensitive JWT secret is stored directly in source code.
column9: Move secrets to environment variables.

column4: Access Token Lifetime Too Long
column5: Access tokens remain valid longer than necessary.
column9: Reduce token expiration time.

column4: Refresh Token Blacklisting Not Enabled
column5: Revoked refresh tokens can still be reused.
column9: Enable token blacklist functionality.

column4: Unauthorized Access Due to Missing Permission Check
column5: Protected endpoints lack permission enforcement.
column9: Apply appropriate permission classes.

column4: Incorrect JWT Header Format
column5: Authorization header does not follow Bearer token format.
column9: Use Authorization: Bearer token.

column4: JWT Token Not Included in Authorization Header
column5: Authenticated requests are sent without tokens.
column9: Attach valid JWT tokens to requests.

column4: User Authentication Bypass Through Anonymous Access
column5: Anonymous users can access protected resources.
column9: Restrict access using authentication checks.

column4: Invalid Token Decoding Exception Unhandled
column5: Token decoding errors cause server failures.
column9: Handle token exceptions gracefully.

column4: Missing Authentication Backend Configuration
column5: Authentication backend is not properly configured.
column9: Configure the correct authentication backend.

column4: Weak Secret Key Used for JWT Signing
column5: JWT secret lacks sufficient complexity.
column9: Use a strong randomly generated secret.

column4: JWT Audience Validation Missing
column5: Token audience claims are not validated.
column9: Validate the audience claim during verification.

column4: JWT Issuer Validation Not Configured
column5: Issuer claims are ignored during validation.
column9: Validate issuer claims against trusted values.

column4: Token Refresh Endpoint Exposed Without Rate Limiting
column5: Refresh endpoint can be abused through repeated requests.
column9: Apply rate limiting to token endpoints.

column4: User Session Remains Active After Logout
column5: Users can continue using tokens after logout.
column9: Revoke or blacklist tokens during logout.

column4: Access Token Accepted After Expiration
column5: Expired access tokens continue to be accepted.
column9: Verify expiration timestamps strictly.

column4: Permission Class Misconfigured in API Settings
column5: Invalid permission class path causes authorization issues.
column9: Use valid DRF permission classes.

column4: Missing Role-Based Access Control
column5: User roles are not enforced consistently.
column9: Implement role-based permissions.

column4: Incorrect User Role Assignment
column5: Users receive unintended permissions.
column9: Validate role assignment logic.

column4: Privilege Escalation Through Improper Authorization
column5: Users can access higher-privilege functionality.
column9: Enforce authorization checks on sensitive actions.

column4: JWT Claims Not Properly Validated
column5: Claims are trusted without verification.
column9: Validate all required JWT claims.

column4: Sensitive Data Stored in JWT Payload
column5: Confidential information is embedded in JWT tokens.
column9: Store only minimal non-sensitive claims.

column4: Missing HTTPS Enforcement for Token Transmission
column5: Tokens may be transmitted over insecure channels.
column9: Enforce HTTPS for all authenticated requests.

column4: Authentication Middleware Not Registered
column5: Authentication middleware is missing from configuration.
column9: Register the required middleware.

column4: CSRF Protection Disabled for Authenticated Endpoints
column5: Authenticated endpoints lack CSRF safeguards.
column9: Enable CSRF protection where applicable.

column4: Insecure Password Storage Configuration
column5: Passwords may not be hashed securely.
column9: Use strong password hashing algorithms.

column4: User Enumeration Through Login Endpoint
column5: Login responses reveal valid usernames.
column9: Return generic authentication error messages.

column4: Brute Force Protection Missing on Authentication API
column5: Repeated login attempts are not restricted.
column9: Add throttling and account lockout measures.

column4: Authentication Error Returns Sensitive Information
column5: Error messages expose internal details.
column9: Sanitize authentication error responses.

column4: Unhandled Token Blacklist Lookup Failure
column5: Blacklist lookup errors crash authentication flow.
column9: Handle blacklist lookup failures safely.

column4: Invalid User Object Returned During Authentication
column5: Authentication process returns incorrect user instances.
column9: Validate user retrieval logic.

column4: Custom JWT Serializer Missing Required Claims
column5: Generated tokens omit required claims.
column9: Include all mandatory claims in serializers.

column4: Token Generation Fails for Inactive Users
column5: Inactive users trigger token generation errors.
column9: Handle inactive accounts explicitly.

column4: JWT Refresh Endpoint Accepts Invalid Tokens
column5: Malformed refresh tokens are accepted.
column9: Validate token integrity before refresh.

column4: Duplicate User Registration Due to Missing Validation
column5: Duplicate accounts can be created.
column9: Add uniqueness validation checks.

column4: Password Reset Token Expiration Not Enforced
column5: Expired password reset tokens remain usable.
column9: Verify expiration before accepting reset tokens.

column4: Incorrect JWT Algorithm Configuration
column5: Application uses an unexpected signing algorithm.
column9: Configure and enforce approved algorithms.

column4: Hardcoded Database Credentials in Python Settings
column5: Database credentials are embedded in code.
column9: Move credentials to secure configuration.

column4: Environment Variables Not Loaded Properly
column5: Required environment variables are unavailable.
column9: Verify environment loading configuration.

column4: Missing Exception Handling in API View
column5: Unhandled exceptions result in server errors.
column9: Add structured exception handling.

column4: Database Connection Leak in Django Application
column5: Connections are not released properly.
column9: Ensure connections are closed after use.

column4: Circular Import Causing Application Startup Failure
column5: Circular dependencies prevent startup.
column9: Refactor imports to remove dependency cycles.

column4: Unvalidated User Input Leading to Server Error
column5: Input is processed without validation.
column9: Validate and sanitize all user input.

column4: API Endpoint Missing Authentication Requirement
column5: Public access is allowed unintentionally.
column9: Require authentication for protected endpoints.

column4: Incorrect Serializer Field Validation
column5: Serializer validation rules are incomplete.
column9: Define proper validation constraints.

column4: Race Condition During User Account Creation
column5: Concurrent requests create inconsistent user data.
column9: Use transactions and uniqueness constraints.

column4: Missing Logging for Authentication Failures
column5: Authentication failures are not logged.
column9: Implement structured security logging.

column4: DEBUG Mode Enabled in Production
column5: Django DEBUG=True set in production exposing detailed error pages with source code.
column9: Set DEBUG=False in production settings. Use environment variable to control DEBUG setting.

column4: ALLOWED_HOSTS Not Configured in Production
column5: ALLOWED_HOSTS set to wildcard in production allowing HTTP Host header attacks.
column9: Set ALLOWED_HOSTS to explicit list of production domains. Never use wildcard in production.

column4: CORS Not Configured for API Endpoints
column5: CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS not set causing frontend API calls to fail with CORS policy errors.
column9: Install django-cors-headers. Set CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to list of frontend domains.

column4: Static Files Not Collected in Production
column5: Static files return 404 in production because collectstatic was not run during deployment.
column9: Run python manage.py collectstatic during deployment pipeline. Configure STATIC_ROOT correctly.

column4: Database Connection Pool Not Configured
column5: Django creating a new database connection for each request causing connection exhaustion under load.
column9: Configure CONN_MAX_AGE in DATABASE settings. Use PgBouncer for connection pooling.

column4: Celery Broker URL Using Default Redis Database
column5: Celery using default Redis database 0 shared with other services causing task interference.
column9: Set a dedicated Redis database number in CELERY_BROKER_URL.

column4: Missing SECRET_KEY Rotation After Exposure
column5: Django SECRET_KEY unchanged after being accidentally committed to version control.
column9: Immediately rotate SECRET_KEY. Set SECRET_KEY from environment variable. Scan git history.

column4: Email Backend Set to Console in Production
column5: EMAIL_BACKEND set to console backend in production causing emails to not be sent.
column9: Set EMAIL_BACKEND to smtp or third-party provider backend in production.

column4: Missing HTTPS Redirect Middleware
column5: Application serves responses over HTTP in production without redirecting to HTTPS.
column9: Add SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=True in production settings. Add SecurityMiddleware to MIDDLEWARE.

column4: Incorrect Time Zone Setting
column5: Django TIME_ZONE set to UTC but business logic assumes local timezone causing scheduling errors.
column9: Set TIME_ZONE to correct business timezone. Enable USE_TZ=True for timezone-aware datetimes.

column4: Cache Backend Not Configured
column5: Django using dummy cache backend in production causing performance issues.
column9: Configure CACHES to use Redis or Memcached in production.

column4: Missing Database Index on Frequently Queried Field
column5: Foreign key field used in frequent filter queries has no database index causing full table scans.
column9: Add db_index=True to frequently queried fields. Create migration to add index.

column4: Logging Not Configured for Production
column5: Django logging uses default configuration causing important errors to be silently discarded.
column9: Configure LOGGING in settings with appropriate handlers for production.

column4: Media Files Served by Django in Production
column5: Django configured to serve MEDIA_FILES directly in production causing performance bottleneck.
column9: Serve media files via nginx S3 or CDN in production. Configure MEDIA_URL to external storage URL.

column4: Session Engine Using Database Without Cleanup
column5: SESSION_ENGINE set to database sessions but expired sessions never cleaned up causing table bloat.
column9: Run clearsessions periodically via cron or Celery beat.

column4: Missing CSRF Cookie Settings for SPA
column5: CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True prevents JavaScript from reading CSRF token breaking SPA authentication.
column9: Set CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=False for SPA applications that need to read CSRF token via JavaScript.

column4: Gunicorn Worker Count Not Optimized
column5: Gunicorn running with only 1 worker in production causing requests to queue under load.
column9: Set workers=(2*CPU_COUNT)+1 as per Gunicorn docs. Configure via environment variable.

column4: Redis Connection Not Using SSL in Production
column5: Redis connection in production uses unencrypted connection exposing data in transit.
column9: Use rediss:// scheme in Redis URL for SSL connections.

column4: Incorrect WSGI Application Path
column5: WSGI_APPLICATION setting points to incorrect module path causing ImportError on application start.
column9: Verify WSGI_APPLICATION matches actual wsgi.py location.

column4: Missing Health Check Endpoint Configuration
column5: Load balancer health checks return 404 because health check endpoint not configured.
column9: Add health check endpoint to urls.py. Return 200 with simple JSON response.

column4: MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE Not Configured
column5: Django has no upload size limit configured allowing denial of service through large file uploads.
column9: Set DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE and FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE in settings.

column4: Template Caching Disabled in Production
column5: Django template loaders not configured for caching causing repeated disk reads.
column9: Use cached.Loader in TEMPLATES settings for production.

column4: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
column5: X_FRAME_OPTIONS not configured making site vulnerable to clickjacking attacks.
column9: Set X_FRAME_OPTIONS=DENY in settings. Add XFrameOptionsMiddleware to MIDDLEWARE.

column4: Misconfigured Proxy Headers Trust
column5: SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER not configured correctly when behind load balancer.
column9: Set SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER=('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO','https') when behind SSL-terminating proxy.

column4: Worker Timeout Too Short for Long Operations
column5: Gunicorn worker timeout set to 30s causes long-running operations to be killed mid-execution.
column9: Increase timeout for workers handling long operations. Move long tasks to Celery.

column4: Missing Sentry or Error Tracking Configuration
column5: Application has no error tracking configured in production causing errors to go unnoticed.
column9: Integrate Sentry SDK. Set SENTRY_DSN from environment variable.

column4: Django Admin Not Secured on Custom URL
column5: Django admin accessible at default /admin/ URL making it easy target for automated attacks.
column9: Change admin URL to non-default path. Add IP restriction middleware for admin access.

column4: Missing Content Security Policy Header
column5: CSP header not configured allowing any external scripts to load on the page.
column9: Configure CSP header using django-csp library. Start with report-only mode.

column4: Session Cookie Domain Not Set
column5: SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN not configured for subdomain access causing session not to persist.
column9: Set SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN='.yourdomain.com' to share session across subdomains.

column4: Missing Database SSL Configuration
column5: Database connection in production does not use SSL transmitting data in plaintext.
column9: Add OPTIONS: sslmode: require to DATABASE configuration.

column4: Incorrectly Configured URL Patterns Causing 404
column5: URL patterns have trailing slash mismatch causing requests to return 404.
column9: Enable APPEND_SLASH=True in settings. Use consistent URL patterns.

column4: Missing Pagination Configuration in DRF
column5: DEFAULT_PAGINATION_CLASS not set causing all list endpoints to return unbounded results.
column9: Set DEFAULT_PAGINATION_CLASS and PAGE_SIZE in REST_FRAMEWORK settings.

column4: Internationalization Settings Misconfigured
column5: LANGUAGE_CODE and LOCALE_PATHS not configured correctly causing translations to not load.
column9: Set LANGUAGE_CODE to target language. Add locale directory to LOCALE_PATHS.

column4: Missing Rate Limiting Configuration
column5: No throttling classes configured in DEFAULT_THROTTLE_CLASSES leaving API open to abuse.
column9: Configure DEFAULT_THROTTLE_CLASSES and DEFAULT_THROTTLE_RATES in REST_FRAMEWORK settings.

column4: Test Runner Not Excluded from Production
column5: Django test database created in production due to incorrect settings inheritance.
column9: Keep test settings separate from production. Never run tests against production database.

column4: Missing Backup Configuration
column5: Database backups not configured risking complete data loss on hardware failure.
column9: Configure automated database backups. Store backups in separate location. Test restore procedure.

column4: Incorrect File Permission on Settings File
column5: settings.py file has world-readable permissions exposing secrets to other system users.
column9: Set file permissions to 600 on settings files. Move secrets to environment variables.

column4: Missing Robots.txt Configuration
column5: No robots.txt configured allowing search engines to index API and admin pages.
column9: Create robots.txt disallowing indexing of /api/ /admin/ and other non-public endpoints.

column4: Incorrect Celery Task Queue Configuration
column5: Celery tasks routed to wrong queue due to misconfigured CELERY_TASK_ROUTES.
column9: Define explicit CELERY_TASK_ROUTES mapping task names to queues.

column4: Missing SSL Certificate Renewal Configuration
column5: SSL certificate expiry not monitored or auto-renewed causing unexpected HTTPS outage.
column9: Configure auto-renewal with Let's Encrypt certbot. Set up monitoring for certificate expiry.

column4: Default Django Secret Key Used in Repository
column5: Default or example SECRET_KEY from Django template used in production.
column9: Generate new SECRET_KEY. Never use default keys. Load from environment variable.

column4: Missing Middleware Order Configuration
column5: SecurityMiddleware not first in MIDDLEWARE list causing security headers to not be applied.
column9: Place SecurityMiddleware as first item in MIDDLEWARE list.

column4: Connection Timeout Not Configured for External Services
column5: HTTP connections to external services have no timeout configured causing requests to hang.
column9: Set timeout parameter on all requests calls. Use (connect_timeout, read_timeout) tuple.

column4: Missing Migrations in Deployment
column5: Database schema changes deployed without running migrations causing IntegrityError.
column9: Add python manage.py migrate to deployment pipeline before starting application.

column4: Incorrect Timezone in Scheduled Tasks
column5: Celery beat tasks scheduled with naive datetimes causing incorrect execution after DST changes.
column9: Use timezone-aware datetimes in Celery beat schedule. Set CELERY_TIMEZONE in settings.

column4: Missing Environment-Specific Settings Override
column5: Production deployment loads development settings enabling DEBUG mode.
column9: Use DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE environment variable to specify correct settings file.

column4: Incorrect MEDIA_ROOT Configuration
column5: MEDIA_ROOT points to non-existent or web-inaccessible directory causing media upload failures.
column9: Verify MEDIA_ROOT directory exists and is writable by application user.

column4: Missing Database Read Replica Configuration
column5: All queries hitting primary database causing read performance issues.
column9: Configure DATABASE_ROUTERS to route read queries to replica.

column4: API Versioning Not Configured in URL Patterns
column5: API lacks versioning making backward-incompatible changes break existing clients.
column9: Add API version prefix to URL patterns (/api/v1/). Configure DEFAULT_VERSION in DRF settings.

column4: Missing Static File Compression Configuration
column5: Static files served without compression increasing page load times and bandwidth usage.
column9: Configure WhiteNoise with compression settings. Or use nginx gzip compression.

column4: PostgreSQL Max Connections Exceeded
column5: Application exhausts PostgreSQL max_connections limit causing new connections to fail.
column9: Use PgBouncer connection pooler. Configure CONN_MAX_AGE. Monitor connection count with pg_stat_activity.

column4: N+1 Query Problem in Serializer
column5: Serializer accessing related objects in loop generates N+1 database queries.
column9: Use select_related() for ForeignKey. Use prefetch_related() for ManyToMany. Profile with django-debug-toolbar.

column4: Database Migration Fails Due to Existing Data
column5: Migration adding NOT NULL column fails on table with existing rows.
column9: Add default value or null=True when adding columns to tables with existing data.

column4: Deadlock Detected in Concurrent Update Operations
column5: Concurrent requests updating same rows in different order cause database deadlock.
column9: Order database operations consistently. Use SELECT FOR UPDATE with NOWAIT. Implement retry logic.

column4: Database Query Timeout in Complex Report
column5: Complex aggregation query times out after 30 seconds.
column9: Add statement_timeout. Move complex reports to async Celery tasks. Create materialized views.

column4: Unicode Characters Cause Database Encoding Error
column5: Emoji and special Unicode characters in user input cause encoding error when storing.
column9: Ensure PostgreSQL database uses UTF8 encoding. Set client_encoding=UTF8 in connection settings.

column4: Missing Foreign Key Index Causing Slow Joins
column5: Frequently joined table missing foreign key index causes sequential scans on every join.
column9: Add db_index=True to ForeignKey fields used in joins. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on slow queries.

column4: Transaction Not Atomic Causing Partial Data Insert
column5: Multi-step database operation not wrapped in transaction causing partial data insertion.
column9: Wrap related database operations in @transaction.atomic decorator.

column4: Database Backup Restore Fails Due to Schema Mismatch
column5: Restoring backup to new environment fails because migration history does not match.
column9: Use pg_dump with portable flags. Test restore procedure in staging environment regularly.

column4: Query Using LIKE with Leading Wildcard Not Using Index
column5: Search query using LIKE '%keyword%' pattern cannot use standard btree index.
column9: Use PostgreSQL full-text search SearchVector and SearchQuery. Or use trigram index.

column4: Django ORM Silently Truncating String Data
column5: CharField with max_length silently truncates input in MySQL causing data loss.
column9: Enable strict mode in MySQL. Add explicit length validation in serializer.

column4: Database Connection Drops Under High Load
column5: PostgreSQL connections dropped intermittently under load due to TCP keepalive not configured.
column9: Set TCP keepalive options in DATABASE OPTIONS. Configure PostgreSQL tcp_keepalives_idle.

column4: Slow Query Due to Missing Composite Index
column5: Query filtering on two columns separately not using available single-column indexes.
column9: Create composite index covering both filter columns. Use Index Meta option in Django model.

column4: Cascade Delete Removes Unintended Related Records
column5: ForeignKey with on_delete=CASCADE deletes related records unintentionally.
column9: Review on_delete behavior for all ForeignKey fields. Use PROTECT to prevent unintended deletion.

column4: Database Timezone Mismatch Causing Wrong Timestamps
column5: PostgreSQL server timezone differs from Django TIME_ZONE causing timestamp errors.
column9: Set PostgreSQL timezone to UTC. Use Django timezone-aware datetime handling.

column4: Row-Level Lock Timeout Causing Request Failure
column5: SELECT FOR UPDATE on busy row causes lock wait timeout returning 500.
column9: Set lock_timeout in Django database settings. Implement retry logic for lock timeouts.

column4: Missing Database Constraint Allowing Duplicate Records
column5: Unique constraint not added at database level relying only on application-level validation.
column9: Add unique_together or UniqueConstraint in model Meta. Handle IntegrityError in view.

column4: Full Table Scan on Large Table Causing Timeout
column5: Admin query against table with millions of rows performing full table scan.
column9: Add index to filter fields on large tables. Use cursor pagination instead of offset.

column4: Database Schema Drift Between Environments
column5: Production database schema differs from development due to manually applied changes.
column9: Never make manual schema changes. Use Django migrations exclusively.

column4: Bulk Insert Causing Memory Error
column5: Bulk create with large queryset loaded entirely into memory causes OOM error.
column9: Use bulk_create with batch_size parameter. Process bulk operations in chunks.

column4: Raw SQL Query Missing Parameter Binding
column5: Raw SQL query uses Python string formatting instead of parameterized queries enabling SQL injection.
column9: Use cursor.execute(sql, params) with parameterized queries. Never use string formatting in raw SQL.

column4: Database User Has Excessive Privileges
column5: Application database user has superuser privileges creating significant security risk.
column9: Create dedicated database user with minimal required privileges.

column4: Inefficient Queryset Evaluation in Template
column5: Queryset passed to template is evaluated multiple times.
column9: Evaluate queryset once and pass list to template. Use list() on queryset to force evaluation.

column4: Missing Soft Delete Implementation
column5: Hard deletes remove records permanently causing foreign key errors and loss of audit trail.
column9: Implement soft delete with is_deleted flag and deleted_at timestamp.

column4: Database Connection String Contains Password in Logs
column5: Database URL with embedded password logged in application startup logs.
column9: Never log DATABASE_URL or connection strings. Mask password in log output.

column4: Missing Read-After-Write Consistency
column5: User creates record then immediately queries it but replica returns stale data.
column9: Route writes and immediate reads to primary database.

column4: Integer Primary Key Approaching Maximum Value
column5: Auto-increment integer primary key approaching maximum value for high-volume table.
column9: Migrate to BigAutoField. Check current max ID value. Plan migration before running out of IDs.

column4: Queryset Filter Using Python Instead of Database
column5: Filtering performed by loading all records into Python and filtering in memory.
column9: Always filter using queryset methods which generate SQL WHERE clauses.

column4: Missing Database Vacuum on Large Delete Operations
column5: Frequent large deletes causing table bloat due to PostgreSQL MVCC not reclaiming space.
column9: Run VACUUM ANALYZE on tables with frequent deletes. Configure autovacuum thresholds.

column4: Incorrect Use of .save() in Loop Causing N+1 Writes
column5: Updating records in loop with .save() causes N individual UPDATE queries.
column9: Use bulk_update() for multiple record updates. Or use update() on queryset.

column4: Missing Database Replication Lag Monitoring
column5: Application does not monitor replication lag causing stale reads during high write load.
column9: Monitor replication lag metric. Implement lag-aware routing.

column4: JSON Field Queries Not Using GIN Index
column5: Queries filtering on JSONField attributes performing full table scans.
column9: Create GIN index on JSONField using GinIndex in model Meta.

column4: Missing Database Audit Log for Sensitive Tables
column5: Changes to sensitive tables not logged preventing security incident investigation.
column9: Implement audit logging using django-auditlog or custom signal handlers.

column4: Database Migration Lock Causing Downtime
column5: Running migration that acquires exclusive lock on large table causes unavailability.
column9: Use concurrent index creation for large tables. Split migrations into non-locking steps.

column4: ORM Query Generating Cartesian Product
column5: Incorrect use of filter across multiple ManyToMany relations generates cartesian product.
column9: Use distinct() after filtering across multiple ManyToMany relations.

column4: Missing Prepared Statement Caching
column5: Application not using prepared statements causing query plan generation overhead.
column9: Enable prepared statement caching in psycopg2 connection settings.

column4: Database Column Renamed Without Backward-Compatible Migration
column5: Column rename migration breaks running application instances still using old column name.
column9: Add new column first copy data update application then remove old column in separate deployment.

column4: Missing Partial Index for Soft-Deleted Records
column5: Full index on is_active column includes all deleted records causing inefficient queries.
column9: Create partial index WHERE is_deleted=False for queries that always filter active records.

column4: Float Field Used for Currency Storage
column5: Monetary values stored in Float field causing floating-point precision errors.
column9: Use DecimalField with appropriate max_digits and decimal_places for all monetary values.

column4: Missing Database Connection Retry Logic
column5: Application fails immediately on transient database connection error instead of retrying.
column9: Implement connection retry logic with exponential backoff. Use tenacity library.

column4: Third-Party API Rate Limit Not Handled
column5: Application makes unrestricted calls to payment API hitting rate limit.
column9: Implement rate limit tracking and request queuing. Use exponential backoff on 429 responses.

column4: Webhook Signature Validation Missing
column5: Incoming webhooks accepted without validating HMAC signature enabling fraudulent injection.
column9: Validate webhook signature using HMAC-SHA256. Reject webhooks with invalid signatures.

column4: API Timeout Not Configured for External Service Call
column5: HTTP request to external API has no timeout causing thread to hang indefinitely.
column9: Set timeout on all requests calls. Implement circuit breaker pattern.

column4: SSL Certificate Not Verified on Outbound API Call
column5: Application makes outbound API calls with verify=False disabling SSL certificate validation.
column9: Never disable SSL verification. Set verify=True or path to CA bundle.

column4: API Response Not Cached - Repeated Identical Requests
column5: Identical API requests made repeatedly without caching increasing costs and latency.
column9: Cache API responses using Redis with appropriate TTL.

column4: Missing Circuit Breaker for Downstream Service
column5: Application continues making calls to failing downstream service causing cascade failure.
column9: Implement circuit breaker using pybreaker. Return cached response when circuit is open.

column4: API Version Mismatch Causing Deserialization Failure
column5: External API upgraded version changing response schema. Application still using old schema.
column9: Pin API version in request URL. Handle missing fields with .get() and defaults.

column4: Authentication Token Not Refreshed Before API Call
column5: External API token expires during long operation causing subsequent calls to fail.
column9: Check token expiry before each API call. Refresh token proactively before expiry.

column4: Missing Retry Logic for Transient API Errors
column5: Transient 500 errors from external API immediately returned to user without retry.
column9: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for 5xx errors. Use tenacity library.

column4: API Response Parsing Assumes Fixed Schema
column5: Application breaks when API adds new fields or returns optional fields as null.
column9: Use defensive parsing with .get() and default values. Use Pydantic for schema validation.

column4: Sensitive Data Logged From API Response
column5: Full API response including PII and payment details logged for debugging.
column9: Never log full API responses. Mask sensitive fields before logging.

column4: Missing Idempotency Key on Payment API Call
column5: Payment API called without idempotency key risking duplicate charges on network retry.
column9: Generate unique idempotency key per payment attempt. Pass in request header.

column4: Third-Party API Error Not Mapped to Internal Error Code
column5: Raw error responses from third-party APIs returned directly to clients.
column9: Map external API errors to internal error codes. Never expose vendor-specific errors.

column4: API Integration Test Using Production Environment
column5: Integration tests running against live production external API incurring costs.
column9: Use sandbox environments for integration tests. Mock external API calls in unit tests.

column4: Missing Correlation ID in API Requests
column5: Outbound API requests lack correlation ID header making request tracing impossible.
column9: Generate correlation ID at request entry point. Pass as X-Correlation-ID header.

column4: Pagination Not Implemented for API List Endpoint
column5: Application fetches all records from external API without pagination causing timeout.
column9: Implement pagination using page or cursor-based approach. Handle has_more indicators.

column4: OAuth Scope Requesting Excessive Permissions
column5: Application requests all available OAuth scopes violating principle of least privilege.
column9: Request only OAuth scopes required for application functionality.

column4: API Key Stored in Client-Side Code
column5: API key for backend service embedded in frontend JavaScript exposing it to all users.
column9: Never store API keys in frontend code. Create backend proxy endpoint.

column4: Missing Exponential Backoff on Rate Limit Response
column5: Application retries immediately on 429 response causing rapid retry loop.
column9: Respect Retry-After header. Implement exponential backoff with jitter.

column4: JSON Parse Error Not Handled on API Response
column5: API sometimes returns HTML error page instead of JSON causing JSONDecodeError.
column9: Check Content-Type header before parsing. Handle JSONDecodeError exception gracefully.

column4: GraphQL Query Depth Not Limited
column5: GraphQL API allows infinitely nested queries enabling DoS attacks.
column9: Implement query depth limiting. Set maximum query complexity. Disable introspection in production.

column4: Missing API Response Validation
column5: API response from external service used without validation causing errors on schema mismatch.
column9: Validate API response against expected schema using Pydantic models.

column4: Synchronous API Call Blocking Request Thread
column5: Long-running external API call executed synchronously blocking Django thread.
column9: Move external API calls to async Celery tasks. Use async Django views with httpx.

column4: Missing Vendor API Credential Rotation
column5: Third-party API credentials never rotated increasing risk of long-term compromise.
column9: Implement regular API credential rotation schedule. Store credentials in secrets manager.

column4: API Response Headers Not Respected
column5: Application ignores Cache-Control and ETag headers from API making unnecessary requests.
column9: Implement HTTP caching respecting Cache-Control and ETag headers.

column4: Batch API Request Not Implemented
column5: Application makes individual API call per record instead of using available batch endpoint.
column9: Use batch API endpoints where available. Group individual requests into batches.

column4: Missing Webhook Retry Handling
column5: Application does not handle duplicate webhook deliveries processing same event multiple times.
column9: Implement idempotent webhook processing. Track processed webhook IDs in database.

column4: API Error Message Shown Directly to User
column5: Raw error message from third-party payment API displayed directly to end user.
column9: Translate API errors to user-friendly messages. Log technical details internally.

column4: Missing API Documentation for Internal Endpoints
column5: Internal API endpoints lack documentation causing integration errors from other teams.
column9: Add drf-spectacular for automatic OpenAPI documentation generation.

column4: Third-Party Service Outage Not Handled Gracefully
column5: Application returns 500 when third-party payment service is down.
column9: Implement fallback behavior for critical external service outages. Queue failed operations.

column4: API Response Deserialization Using Eval
column5: API response parsed using eval() for speed enabling code injection if response is compromised.
column9: Never use eval() for JSON parsing. Use json.loads() exclusively.

column4: Missing API Usage Metrics
column5: No metrics collected for external API calls including latency and error breakdown.
column9: Instrument all external API calls with metrics using Prometheus or StatsD.

column4: API Integration Not Tested in Staging
column5: External API integration only tested in production due to missing staging credentials.
column9: Set up sandbox accounts for all third-party APIs. Run integration tests in CI pipeline.

column4: Outbound API Request Not Audited
column5: No audit log maintained for outbound API calls involving user data.
column9: Log all outbound API calls with timestamp endpoint user context and outcome.

column4: Stale API Token Cache Not Invalidated
column5: Cached API token not invalidated after manual revocation causing use of revoked token.
column9: Implement cache invalidation on token refresh or error 401 response from downstream.

column4: Missing Content-Type Header on API Request
column5: Outbound API request missing Content-Type header causing 415 Unsupported Media Type error.
column9: Set Content-Type header on all POST/PUT/PATCH requests. Use session.headers.update().

column4: API Response Cached Without Considering User Context
column5: API response cached globally without considering user-specific data causing data leakage.
column9: Include user identifier in cache key for user-specific API responses.

column4: Missing Fallback for Optional Third-Party Feature
column5: Application crashes when optional third-party analytics service is unavailable.
column9: Wrap optional service calls in try-except. Log failures but continue normal operation.

column4: Incorrect HTTP Method Used for API Endpoint
column5: Application uses GET request for operation that modifies data violating REST semantics.
column9: Use appropriate HTTP methods. Fix incorrect method usage. Update API documentation.

column4: Missing API Contract Testing
column5: No contract tests between API producer and consumer causing breaking changes undetected.
column9: Implement consumer-driven contract testing using Pact. Run contract tests in CI pipeline.

column4: Staff User Accessing Admin-Only Endpoint
column5: Staff users with is_staff=True can access admin-only endpoints restricted to superusers.
column9: Create custom permission class IsSuperUser checking request.user.is_superuser.

column4: Role Check Missing on Bulk Delete Endpoint
column5: Bulk delete endpoint only checks authentication not user role.
column9: Add role-based permission class to bulk delete endpoint. Restrict to admin role only.

column4: Object-Level Permission Not Enforced
column5: List-level permission passes but individual object access not validated.
column9: Implement get_object() override to call check_object_permissions(). Return 404 instead of 403.

column4: Permission Caching Causing Stale Access
column5: Role changes not reflected immediately because permissions cached in JWT payload.
column9: Implement permission cache invalidation on role change. Use short-lived JWTs with database check.

column4: Owner-Only Resource Accessible by Any Authenticated User
column5: User documents accessible by any authenticated user knowing the document ID.
column9: Add owner check in queryset: queryset.filter(owner=request.user).

column4: RBAC Roles Not Validated at Model Level
column5: Permission checks exist in views but model methods perform sensitive operations without role check.
column9: Implement permission checks at model or service layer not just view layer.

column4: Superuser Permission Bypasses All Security Checks
column5: Superuser flag bypasses all custom permission checks.
column9: Do not rely on is_superuser bypass for all checks. Implement explicit admin permissions.

column4: Permission Denied Returns Wrong Status Code
column5: Application returns 500 instead of 403 when permission check raises unexpected exception.
column9: Catch all exceptions in permission check and return PermissionDenied.

column4: Group-Based Permissions Not Inherited Correctly
column5: User in multiple groups gets incorrect effective permissions due to AND logic instead of OR.
column9: Use OR logic for group permission aggregation. User should get union of all group permissions.

column4: Temporary Permission Elevation Not Expiring
column5: Temporary admin access granted for support purposes is never revoked.
column9: Implement time-limited permission grants with automatic expiry.

column4: Read Permission Checked But Write Not
column5: View applies read permission check but update endpoint accessible without write permission.
column9: Apply appropriate permission for each HTTP method. Map GET to read POST to write.

column4: Permission Check Runs After Data Fetch
column5: View fetches data before checking permissions causing unnecessary DB load.
column9: Check permissions before fetching data. Override initial() method in viewset.

column4: Cross-Tenant Data Access Not Prevented
column5: Multi-tenant application does not filter querysets by tenant.
column9: Filter all querysets by current user tenant. Implement tenant-aware base queryset.

column4: Admin Action Available to All Authenticated Users
column5: Django admin custom action not protected with admin-only restriction.
column9: Override has_permission in admin class to check is_staff or is_superuser.

column4: IP Whitelist Permission Not Applied to All Methods
column5: IP whitelist only checked for POST requests allowing GET requests from any IP.
column9: Apply IP whitelist check to all HTTP methods. Implement as middleware.

column4: Role Assigned Based on User Input
column5: User role assigned based on role field in registration request body.
column9: Never trust role values from user input. Assign roles server-side based on business logic.

column4: Nested Resource Permission Not Checked
column5: Access to /api/projects/{id}/tasks/ checks project permission but not task access.
column9: Check permissions at each resource level. Validate user access to both project and task.

column4: Permission Check Not Thread-Safe
column5: Custom permission class uses shared mutable state causing wrong result under concurrency.
column9: Make permission classes stateless. Store permission context in request scope only.

column4: Soft Delete Bypass via Deleted Record Access
column5: Soft-deleted records accessible via direct ID even though filtered from list endpoints.
column9: Include is_deleted=False in get_queryset() not just list actions.

column4: Department Isolation Not Enforced on Reports
column5: Report endpoints return data across all departments not filtered to user department.
column9: Implement department-based queryset filtering. Add department to user model.

column4: Missing Permission Check on Password Change Endpoint
column5: Password change endpoint allows any authenticated user to change any user password.
column9: Verify request.user matches user_id in password change request.

column4: API Allows Modification of Own Role
column5: User account update endpoint allows users to modify their own role field.
column9: Remove role from user-editable fields. Create separate admin-only role management endpoint.

column4: Missing Permission on Aggregate Endpoint
column5: Aggregate statistics endpoint returns all users data without checking admin role.
column9: Add admin role check on aggregate endpoints.

column4: Permission Logging Not Implemented
column5: No audit log of permission denied events making security investigation impossible.
column9: Log all permission denied events with user resource action and timestamp.

column4: Anonymous User Accessing Restricted Statistics
column5: Statistics endpoint accessible without authentication returns aggregate metrics.
column9: Add authentication requirement to all statistics endpoints.

column4: View Permission Allows Execute Action
column5: User with view-only role can trigger write operations through undocumented action endpoint.
column9: Map custom actions to appropriate permission levels. Test all custom actions with minimum role.

column4: Missing Field-Level Permission Control
column5: Read permission restricts record access but sensitive fields exposed to unauthorized users.
column9: Implement field-level permissions. Return different serializer based on user role.

column4: Impersonation Feature Missing Audit Trail
column5: Support impersonation feature allows admin to act as any user without logging actions.
column9: Log all actions taken during impersonation. Restrict to specific high-privilege admins only.

column4: Batch Operation Not Checking Each Item Permission
column5: Batch update endpoint checks permission once but not for each individual item.
column9: Check permissions for each item in batch operation. Return partial success with failed items.

column4: Time-Based Access Control Not Implemented
column5: System should restrict access outside business hours but permission check does not validate time.
column9: Implement time-based access control in custom permission class.

column4: Horizontal Privilege Escalation via ID Manipulation
column5: User can access other users private data by incrementing ID in URL parameter.
column9: Never expose sequential numeric IDs. Use UUIDs. Always filter querysets by owner.

column4: Missing Permission Check on File Download
column5: File download endpoint checks authentication but not whether user owns the file.
column9: Add ownership check for file download. Return 404 for inaccessible files.

column4: Resource Sharing Not Respecting Recipient Permissions
column5: Shared resource accessible to recipient even after sharing is revoked by owner.
column9: Implement dynamic permission check on each access. Store sharing state in database.

column4: Admin Endpoint Accessible Due to Config Flag Leaked to Production
column5: Admin-only endpoint marked as debug-only but accessible in production due to config flag.
column9: Use explicit allowlist for production endpoints. Never rely on feature flags for security.

column4: Missing Re-Authentication for Sensitive Operations
column5: Sensitive operations like payment proceed without re-authentication.
column9: Require recent authentication for sensitive operations. Check last authentication time.

column4: GraphQL Mutations Not Checking Authorization
column5: GraphQL mutations accessible to any authenticated user without role-based restriction.
column9: Add permission decorators to GraphQL mutations. Test each mutation with different user roles.

column4: Missing Permission Check on Async Task
column5: Celery task triggered from API endpoint does not receive user context.
column9: Pass user ID and permissions to Celery task explicitly. Revalidate permissions at task time.

column4: Wildcard Role Assignment Granting Unintended Access
column5: Wildcard role pattern matching too broadly granting access to unintended resources.
column9: Use explicit resource-action permission mappings instead of wildcards.

column4: Missing Version-Level Permission Check
column5: New API version deployed with relaxed permissions during transition.
column9: Apply consistent permission requirements across all API versions.

column4: Permission Check Skipped for OPTIONS Request
column5: CORS preflight OPTIONS request handled without permission check leaking API structure.
column9: Return minimal response to OPTIONS requests. Do not expose detailed API schema.

column4: Server-Side Request Forgery in URL Fetch Feature
column5: URL fetch feature allows users to specify arbitrary URLs enabling requests to internal services.
column9: Validate and sanitize URLs before fetching. Block requests to private IP ranges. Use URL allowlist.

column4: Command Injection via Filename Parameter
column5: File processing passes filename parameter directly to shell command enabling command injection.
column9: Never pass user input to shell commands. Use subprocess with list argument not shell=True.

column4: Insecure Deserialization of Pickle Data
column5: Application deserializes user-provided pickle data enabling arbitrary code execution.
column9: Never deserialize untrusted pickle data. Use JSON for data serialization between services.

column4: XML Injection in SAML Authentication
column5: SAML assertion parsing vulnerable to XML injection allowing authentication bypass.
column9: Use battle-tested SAML library. Validate signature before processing any assertion content.

column4: Template Injection in Email Template Rendering
column5: User-provided content passed directly to Jinja2 template rendering enabling SSTI.
column9: Never render user input as Jinja2 template. Use sandboxed Jinja2 environment.

column4: Insecure Direct Object Reference in Download
column5: File download URL uses predictable sequential ID allowing enumeration.
column9: Use UUIDs or signed URLs for file references. Validate access rights on every download.

column4: Subdomain Takeover Vulnerability
column5: Expired subdomain still has DNS CNAME record pointing to decommissioned cloud service.
column9: Audit all CNAME records before decommissioning services. Remove DNS records when service terminates.

column4: HTTP Response Splitting via Header Injection
column5: User-controlled data injected into HTTP response headers enabling header injection.
column9: Sanitize all user data before using in HTTP headers. Reject values containing CR/LF characters.

column4: Prototype Pollution in JavaScript API Client
column5: JavaScript API client library vulnerable to prototype pollution enabling property injection.
column9: Update vulnerable JavaScript library. Use Object.create(null) for plain objects.

column4: Unvalidated Redirect After Login
column5: Login success redirect uses return_url parameter without validation enabling open redirect.
column9: Validate redirect URL is on same origin or in allowlist of trusted domains.

column4: Server Information Leakage in Headers
column5: Server response headers include Django version Python version and web server version.
column9: Configure web server to suppress version information in headers.

column4: Stack Trace Exposed in API Error Response
column5: Unhandled exception returns full Python stack trace in API response body.
column9: Set DEBUG=False in production. Implement custom exception handler returning generic error.

column4: Hardcoded Cryptographic Key
column5: AES encryption key hardcoded in source code making all encrypted data compromisable.
column9: Store cryptographic keys in key management service. Never hardcode keys in source.

column4: Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking
column5: WebSocket endpoint does not validate Origin header allowing any website to connect.
column9: Validate Origin header on WebSocket connection. Reject connections from untrusted origins.

column4: Missing Subresource Integrity on External Scripts
column5: External JavaScript libraries loaded without SRI hash enabling supply chain attacks.
column9: Add integrity attribute with SHA-256 hash to all external script tags.

column4: Information Disclosure in 404 Response
column5: Custom 404 handler includes resource path and server configuration details.
column9: Return minimal 404 response without revealing resource path or server details.

column4: Clickjacking Vulnerability on Login Page
column5: Login page lacks X-Frame-Options header allowing iframe embedding for clickjacking.
column9: Set X-FRAME-OPTIONS=DENY. Implement frame-busting JavaScript as defense in depth.

column4: CORS Allowing Null Origin
column5: CORS policy allows null origin sent for file:// and data: URIs enabling local file attacks.
column9: Never add null to CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS. Block requests with Origin: null.

column4: Missing Security Headers
column5: Application lacks HSTS CSP X-Content-Type-Options and Referrer-Policy headers.
column9: Configure all security headers using django-csp. Set SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS.

column4: Weak Random Number Generation
column5: Application uses Python random module for security-sensitive operations like token generation.
column9: Use secrets module for all security-sensitive random generation.

column4: API Endpoint Exposing User Enumeration
column5: User lookup returns 404 for non-existent users and 403 for existing users enabling enumeration.
column9: Return consistent response for both cases. Add artificial delay to prevent timing enumeration.

column4: Insufficient Logging of Security Events
column5: Critical security events like failed logins and permission denials not logged.
column9: Implement structured security logging for all security events. Send logs to SIEM system.

column4: Mass Assignment Vulnerability in Update Endpoint
column5: PUT endpoint updates all fields including read-only fields like is_admin from user JSON body.
column9: Use explicit field whitelisting in serializers. Define read_only_fields for sensitive fields.

column4: Missing Input Sanitization Before RegEx Evaluation
column5: User input used in regular expression without sanitization enabling ReDoS attacks.
column9: Never use user input as regex pattern. Validate regex complexity before evaluation.

column4: Unprotected Admin Password Reset Endpoint
column5: Admin password reset endpoint accessible without authentication.
column9: Require current password or MFA for password reset. Implement rate limiting and IP restrictions.

column4: Cookie Without Secure Flag on HTTPS Site
column5: Cookies set without Secure flag allowing transmission over HTTP connections.
column9: Set SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True and CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE=True for HTTPS deployments.

column4: Missing Anti-CSRF Token on State-Changing Request
column5: Form submission endpoint accepts requests without CSRF token.
column9: Ensure CsrfViewMiddleware in MIDDLEWARE. Use @csrf_protect decorator on views.

column4: Verbose Error Messages Revealing Database Schema
column5: Database error messages including table names included in API response.
column9: Catch database exceptions and return generic error messages. Never expose ORM details.

column4: Missing Rate Limiting on Password Reset Endpoint
column5: Password reset endpoint has no rate limiting enabling email flooding.
column9: Apply rate limiting to password reset endpoint. Limit resets per email address per hour.

column4: Unencrypted PII in Application Logs
column5: User email addresses and IP addresses logged in plaintext.
column9: Implement PII masking in logging configuration. Hash or mask email addresses in logs.

column4: Reflected XSS in Error Parameter
column5: Error message parameter from URL reflected in HTML response without encoding.
column9: HTML-encode all URL parameters before including in response. Use Django template auto-escaping.

column4: Missing File Type Validation Allowing Malicious Upload
column5: File upload accepts any file type based on user-provided MIME type without server-side check.
column9: Validate actual file content using python-magic library not just filename or MIME type.

column4: Insecure Password Reset Token
column5: Password reset token generated using MD5 hash of email and timestamp.
column9: Generate reset tokens using secrets.token_urlsafe(32). Store hashed token in database.

column4: API Exposing Internal Service Architecture
column5: API error responses include internal service names IP addresses and microservice URLs.
column9: Sanitize all error messages to remove internal infrastructure details.

column4: Missing Audit Log for Data Export
column5: Data export operations not logged making it impossible to detect unauthorized bulk extraction.
column9: Log all data export operations with user timestamp records count and data type.

column4: HTTPS Not Enforced on All Endpoints
column5: Some API endpoints accessible over plain HTTP exposing authentication tokens in transit.
column9: Enforce HTTPS for all endpoints using SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=True.

column4: Missing Penetration Test Record
column5: Application has no record of security penetration testing.
column9: Schedule regular penetration tests. Conduct automated DAST scanning in CI pipeline.

column4: Log Injection Vulnerability
column5: User-controlled data written directly to log files without sanitization enabling log injection.
column9: Sanitize user input before logging. Remove or escape newline characters.

column4: Race Condition in Token Validation
column5: JWT token validation and blacklist check performed in separate non-atomic operations.
column9: Perform token validation and blacklist check atomically using Redis SET with NX flag.

column4: Missing Content-Type Sniffing Prevention
column5: X-Content-Type-Options header not set allowing browsers to MIME-sniff responses.
column9: Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header. Use SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF=True.

column4: Unoptimized Database Query on High-Traffic Endpoint
column5: Most-visited endpoint executes 50+ database queries per request due to missing select_related.
column9: Profile queries using django-debug-toolbar. Add select_related and prefetch_related.

column4: Redis Cache Stampede on Cache Miss
column5: Multiple concurrent requests simultaneously hit the database when cache expires.
column9: Implement cache stampede prevention using Redis SET NX lock.

column4: Synchronous File Processing Blocking Web Worker
column5: Large file processing executed synchronously in Django view blocking web worker thread.
column9: Move file processing to Celery task. Return 202 Accepted with task ID.

column4: Missing Database Query Result Caching
column5: Expensive aggregate query recalculated on every page load when data changes infrequently.
column9: Cache query results in Redis with appropriate TTL. Invalidate cache on data change.

column4: Unbounded In-Memory Data Processing
column5: Entire large dataset loaded into Python memory for processing causing OOM error.
column9: Use database-level aggregation. Stream large datasets using QuerySet.iterator().

column4: Session Store Bottleneck Under High Load
column5: Database-backed session store becoming bottleneck under load.
column9: Migrate session backend to Redis. Configure SESSION_ENGINE to use cache backend.

column4: Missing HTTP Response Compression
column5: API responses not compressed sending large JSON payloads unnecessarily.
column9: Enable GZip compression middleware in Django. Configure nginx gzip compression.

column4: Inefficient Serializer Causing Slow API Response
column5: DRF serializer has_many relations loading all nested objects without pagination.
column9: Paginate nested serializer results. Use SerializerMethodField with limited queryset.

column4: Missing Index Causing Full Table Scan on Filter
column5: Commonly filtered field missing database index causing sequential scan on million-row table.
column9: Create index on filtered fields. Analyze slow query log to identify missing indexes.

column4: API Rate Limiting Causing False Throttling
column5: Rate limiter using IP address shared by all users behind corporate NAT.
column9: Use user-level throttling for authenticated users IP-level only for anonymous.

column4: Slow Template Rendering Due to Complex Logic
column5: Django template contains complex business logic and multiple database queries per render.
column9: Move business logic from templates to views. Cache template fragments.

column4: Missing Connection Pooling for External API
column5: External API calls create new TCP connection per request adding overhead.
column9: Use requests.Session() for connection pooling. Use httpx with connection pooling.

column4: Celery Task Queue Backlog Growing Indefinitely
column5: Celery worker processing rate below task submission rate causing unbounded queue growth.
column9: Scale Celery workers horizontally. Profile task processing time. Set up queue depth monitoring.

column4: Database ORM Lazy Loading in Serializer Loop
column5: Accessing related object in serializer forces separate database query per record.
column9: Use prefetch_related or select_related in viewset get_queryset().

column4: Missing Pagination on Large List Endpoint
column5: List endpoint returns all records without pagination returning megabytes of JSON.
column9: Implement pagination using Django REST framework. Set reasonable PAGE_SIZE.

column4: Slow Search Due to Missing Full-Text Index
column5: Text search using LIKE query on unindexed text columns performing full table scan.
column9: Implement PostgreSQL full-text search using SearchVector and SearchQuery.

column4: Memory Leak in Long-Running Celery Worker
column5: Celery worker process memory grows continuously eventually causing OOM kill.
column9: Profile memory usage. Fix object retention. Configure --max-memory-per-child.

column4: Unoptimized Image Serving
column5: Full-resolution images served for thumbnail display causing unnecessary bandwidth usage.
column9: Generate thumbnails at upload time. Serve appropriately sized images for context.

column4: Database Connection Exhaustion Under Load
column5: Application scales horizontally but total database connections exceed PostgreSQL limit.
column9: Implement centralized connection pooling using PgBouncer.

column4: Missing Async Processing for I/O-Bound Operations
column5: I/O-heavy operations executed synchronously blocking Django thread.
column9: Use Django async views for I/O-bound operations. Or offload to Celery tasks.

column4: Large JSON Payload Deserialization Blocking Request
column5: Deserializing megabyte-sized JSON request body blocks request thread for seconds.
column9: Implement request size limits. Process large payloads asynchronously.

column4: Expensive Computation in Request-Response Cycle
column5: Complex ML inference or report generation executed synchronously causing request timeout.
column9: Move expensive computation to async Celery task. Return 202 with job ID.

column4: Missing Cache Warming Strategy
column5: Cold start after deployment causes spike of slow responses while cache is empty.
column9: Implement cache warming job that pre-populates common queries after deployment.

column4: Inefficient String Concatenation in Loop
column5: Building large string by concatenation in Python loop causing O(n²) memory allocation.
column9: Use list comprehension and join() instead of string concatenation.

column4: Static Asset Cache Headers Not Configured
column5: Static files served without cache headers causing browser to re-download on every load.
column9: Set long-lived cache headers for versioned static assets. Use WhiteNoise with WHITENOISE_MAX_AGE.

column4: Database Write Amplification on Update
column5: Updating single field triggers re-saving of entire model including unchanged fields.
column9: Use queryset.update() for single field updates. Use update_fields parameter on save().

column4: Missing Background Job for Notification Sending
column5: Email and push notification sending in request-response cycle adding seconds to response time.
column9: Move notification sending to Celery tasks. Implement notification batching.

column4: Redundant External API Calls in Serializer
column5: Same external API called multiple times for different fields of same object.
column9: Cache external API responses keyed by object ID. Batch external API calls where possible.

column4: Missing Read-Replica for Heavy Read Workload
column5: All read queries hitting primary database causing latency degradation during write-heavy periods.
column9: Configure read replica and route read queries using DATABASE_ROUTERS.

column4: Queryset Evaluated Multiple Times in View
column5: Same queryset evaluated multiple times in view or template causing duplicate database queries.
column9: Cache queryset result by converting to list. Pass list to serializer and template.

column4: Missing CDN for Global API Performance
column5: API served from single region causing high latency for international users.
column9: Implement CDN with edge caching for cacheable API responses.

column4: Slow Admin List View Without Pagination
column5: Django admin list view displays thousands of records without pagination.
column9: Configure list_per_page in ModelAdmin. Use show_full_result_count=False for large tables.

column4: Missing Database Statement Timeout
column5: Long-running database queries can run indefinitely consuming connection.
column9: Set statement_timeout in PostgreSQL settings or Django database OPTIONS.

column4: Inefficient Bulk Data Export
column5: CSV data export processes entire dataset in memory at once causing OOM on large exports.
column9: Stream CSV export using StreamingHttpResponse. Generate rows lazily using generator.

column4: Missing Application Performance Monitoring
column5: No APM configured making performance regressions invisible until users report slowness.
column9: Integrate APM tool. Track P95 and P99 latency per endpoint. Set up alerting.

column4: Thread Pool Exhaustion Under High Concurrency
column5: Django application server thread pool exhausted under high concurrency.
column9: Increase worker count if CPU-bound. Use async workers for I/O-bound applications.

column4: Cache Key Collision Between Environments
column5: Staging and production sharing same Redis instance with same cache keys causing data corruption.
column9: Prefix cache keys with environment name. Use separate Redis databases per environment.

column4: Missing Timeout on Webhook Delivery
column5: Webhook delivery has no timeout causing thread to block when receiving server is slow.
column9: Set explicit timeout on webhook delivery requests. Run webhook delivery in Celery task.

column4: Slow Page Load Due to Synchronous Analytics
column5: Page tracking analytics calls made synchronously before returning response.
column9: Move analytics calls to async JavaScript. Queue analytics events in Redis for background processing.

column4: Memory-Intensive Report Generation Causing OOM
column5: Complex report generation loads entire dataset into pandas DataFrame causing OOM.
column9: Use database aggregation instead of in-memory processing. Stream report generation in chunks.

column4: Application Fails to Start Due to Missing Environment Variable
column5: Application crashes on startup because required DATABASE_URL environment variable not set.
column9: Add startup validation that checks all required environment variables. Provide clear error message.

column4: Docker Container Running as Root User
column5: Application container runs as root violating principle of least privilege.
column9: Add USER directive to Dockerfile. Create application user with minimal permissions.

column4: Blue-Green Deployment Causing Session Loss
column5: Switching to new deployment causes active user sessions to be lost because sessions stored in-process.
column9: Store sessions in external Redis. Configure SESSION_ENGINE to use cache backend.

column4: Docker Image Not Using Specific Tag
column5: Production Dockerfile uses latest tag for base image causing inconsistent builds.
column9: Pin Docker base image to specific version tag or SHA digest. Lock all dependency versions.

column4: Kubernetes Pod Crash Loop Due to Insufficient Memory
column5: Application pod restart looping because memory limit set too low OOM killed on startup.
column9: Profile actual memory usage. Set memory request and limit with 20% headroom.

column4: Missing Health Check Causing Premature Traffic
column5: Kubernetes sends traffic to pod before application is ready causing 503 during rolling deployment.
column9: Implement readiness probe on health check endpoint. Set appropriate initialDelaySeconds.

column4: CI/CD Pipeline Not Running Tests Before Deploy
column5: Application deployed to production without running test suite allowing regressions.
column9: Add test stage to CI/CD pipeline that runs before deployment. Block deployment on test failure.

column4: Environment Variables Hardcoded in Docker Image
column5: Secrets and configuration hardcoded in Dockerfile committed to version control.
column9: Use Docker secrets or environment variable injection at runtime. Never COPY .env into image.

column4: Missing Rolling Update Strategy Causing Downtime
column5: Deployment strategy set to Recreate causing complete outage during deployment.
column9: Set deployment strategy to RollingUpdate. Configure maxSurge and maxUnavailable.

column4: Application Not Handling SIGTERM for Graceful Shutdown
column5: Application terminates immediately on SIGTERM dropping active requests when pod is terminated.
column9: Implement SIGTERM handler to stop accepting new requests and complete in-flight requests.

column4: Missing Resource Limits Causing Node Resource Starvation
column5: Kubernetes pods without resource limits consume entire node resources starving other pods.
column9: Set CPU and memory requests and limits for all containers. Use ResourceQuota at namespace level.

column4: Log Files Growing Without Rotation
column5: Application writes logs directly to files without rotation filling disk and causing failure.
column9: Configure log rotation using logrotate or Python logging RotatingFileHandler.

column4: Missing Secrets Management - Plain Text Secrets in Git
column5: Database passwords and API keys committed to git repository in plaintext.
column9: Remove secrets from git history. Rotate all exposed secrets. Use secrets manager.

column4: Nginx Configuration Serving Application Files
column5: Nginx misconfigured to serve Python application source files as static content.
column9: Configure Nginx to only serve specific static file directories. Audit Nginx configuration.

column4: Application Port Conflict in Production
column5: Application fails to start because configured port already in use by another service.
column9: Use dynamic port assignment or service discovery. Configure application port via environment variable.

column4: Missing Database Migration Check in Deployment
column5: Application deployed with unapplied migrations causing IntegrityError on new features.
column9: Add migration check as pre-deployment step. Block deployment if migrations pending.

column4: SSL Certificate Installed on Wrong Domain
column5: SSL certificate configured for wrong domain causing certificate validation error.
column9: Issue certificate for correct domain. Use wildcard certificate for flexibility.

column4: Container Image Too Large Causing Slow Deployment
column5: Docker image is 2GB due to unnecessary build dependencies included in production image.
column9: Use multi-stage Docker builds. Only include production dependencies in final image.

column4: Missing Backup Before Database Migration
column5: Production database migration run without backup risking data loss if migration fails.
column9: Always backup database before running migrations in production. Test migration rollback.

column4: Inconsistent Python Version Between Dev and Prod
column5: Developer uses Python 3.11 but production runs Python 3.8 causing syntax errors.
column9: Pin Python version in Dockerfile and pyproject.toml. Use .python-version file with pyenv.

column4: Missing Smoke Tests After Deployment
column5: No automated smoke tests run after deployment allowing broken deployments to go undetected.
column9: Implement smoke test suite covering critical user paths. Run automatically after each deployment.

column4: Kubernetes ConfigMap Not Updated with Application
column5: Application code updated but ConfigMap with configuration not updated causing mismatch.
column9: Version ConfigMaps alongside application code. Update ConfigMaps atomically with deployment.

column4: Missing Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
column5: Application does not scale under load because no HPA configured.
column9: Configure HorizontalPodAutoscaler with CPU and memory targets. Test scaling with load testing.

column4: Production Database Seeded with Test Data
column5: Deployment script ran with wrong environment flag seeding production with test data.
column9: Add environment check before running seed commands. Separate seed scripts per environment.

column4: Missing Network Policy in Kubernetes
column5: Kubernetes pods can communicate freely with any other pod violating network segmentation.
column9: Implement NetworkPolicies to restrict pod-to-pod communication. Default deny all.

column4: Deployment Credentials Stored in CI/CD Without Rotation
column5: Long-lived deployment credentials stored as CI/CD secrets and never rotated.
column9: Implement short-lived credentials using OIDC federation. Rotate long-lived credentials regularly.

column4: Missing Rollback Procedure
column5: No documented or automated rollback procedure causing extended outage on failed deployment.
column9: Implement automated rollback on smoke test failure. Document manual rollback steps. Test regularly.

column4: Production Config Not Isolated from Test Config
column5: Test configuration leaks into production environment due to shared settings module.
column9: Use separate configuration files for each environment. Validate critical settings at startup.

column4: Missing Dependency Version Lock File
column5: requirements.txt uses unpinned dependencies causing different versions in dev and prod.
column9: Pin all dependency versions. Use pip-compile to generate locked requirements.

column4: Application Deploy with Incompatible Migration
column5: New code deployed while old migration was still being applied causing API errors.
column9: Use backward-compatible migrations. Test migration concurrently with traffic.

column4: Missing Monitoring and Alerting Configuration
column5: No monitoring configured for production deployment causing silent failures.
column9: Configure monitoring for key metrics. Set up alerting thresholds. Implement on-call rotation.

column4: Container Timezone Misconfiguration
column5: Docker container running in UTC but application assumes local timezone causing scheduling errors.
column9: Set TZ environment variable in Docker container. Use UTC throughout and convert at display layer.

column4: Missing Pre-Production Environment
